chunks.text: Other Profiles of Notable EECS Women - EECS at Berkeley Other Profiles of Notable EECS Women Profiles hosted on other websites Diane Greene (EE M.S. '88) Inspiring technology entrepreneur Dawn Tilbury (EE M.S. '92, Ph.D. '94) Head of the National Science Foundation’s (NSF) Directorate for Engineering Chelsea Finn (EE Ph.D. '18) Pioneer in deep reinforcement learning. Katherine Yelick EECS and Data Science professor, associate dean for research at Berkeley, and senior advisor on computing at LBNL Ruzena Bajcsy EECS professor, computer visionary, and "enemy of the state." Dawn Song (EE Ph.D. '02) EECS professor and privacy expert Bin Yu EECS and Statistics professor who describes the change in her career trajectory as her "most successful failure." Avideh Zakhor EECS professor and 3D computing visionary Susan Graham CS professor and the College of Engineering’s first woman faculty member Tsu-Jae King Li EECS professor and Berkeley Engineering’s first female dean Shafi Goldwasser (CS M.S.’81, Ph.D.’84) CS professor and cryptography pioneer Andrea Goldsmith (EECS B.S. ‘86, M.S. ‘91, Ph.D. ’94) Wireless communications expert Barbara Simons (CS Ph.D.’81) Taking steps to make votes count Josephine Williamson Q&A with the popular and influential EECS director of operations < EECS Notable Women: Cecilia Aragon EECS Notable Women: Melody Ivory > View Open Faculty Positions
